Inscribed_Angle.definition: An inscribed angle is an angle formed in a circle when two chords (or a chord and a tangent, or two tangents) intersect at a point on the circumference of the circle.
property: Inscribed angles subtending the same arc of a circle are equal.
An inscribed angle subtended by a diameter of a circle is 90 degrees.
The relationship between a central angle and an inscribed angle: The measure of a central angle subtending the same arc is equal to twice the measure of the inscribed angle.
The sum of the opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral (a quadrilateral inscribed in a circle) is 180 degrees.

Parallel_Lines.definition: Parallel lines are defined as two lines in the same plane that do not intersect or meet, regardless of how far they are extended.
property: When two lines are parallel, the alternate interior angles formed are equal.
When two lines are parallel, the corresponding angles formed are equal.
When two lines are parallel, the sum of the consecutive interior angles on the same side of the transversal is 180 degrees.

Right_Triangle.definition: A right triangle is a type of triangle that has one of its angles measuring exactly 90 degrees.
property: Pythagorean Theorem: In a right triangle, the square of the length of the hypotenuse is equal to the sum of the squares of the lengths of the other two sides.
Angle Sum Property: Since one angle is 90 degrees, the sum of the other two angles must also be 90 degrees.
Midline property: In a right-angled triangle, the length of the segment from the midpoint of the hypotenuse to the right-angle vertex is half the length of the hypotenuse.

Central_Angle.definition: A central angle in a circle is an angle whose vertex is located at the center of the circle, and its sides (legs) are radii that intersect the circumference of the circle.
property: Measure Equal to Arc's Degree: The measure of a central angle is equal to the measure of the intercepted arc.
The relationship between a central angle and an inscribed angle: The measure of a central angle subtending the same arc is equal to twice the measure of the inscribed angle.
Central angles subtending the same arc are equal.

Similar_Triangle.definition: Similar triangles are a pair or group of triangles that have the same shape, but may differ in size.
property: Corresponding Angles are Equal: In similar triangles, all the corresponding angles are equal.
Proportional Sides: The lengths of corresponding sides in similar triangles are in the same ratio.

Tangent.definition: In geometry and calculus, a tangent line to a curve at a given point is the straight line that "just touches" the curve at that point.
property: A tangent line is perpendicular to the radius (or diameter) passing through the point of tangency, forming a 90-degree angle.
The lengths of two tangent lines drawn from an external point to a circle are equal.

Isosceles_Triangle.definition: An isosceles triangle is a polygon with three edges (sides) and three vertices (corners), with two of the sides being equal in length. The angles opposite these two equal sides are called the base angles and are also equal in measure. The third side of the triangle is known as the "base," and the angle opposite the base is called the "vertex angle" or "apex angle."
property: The two legs of an isosceles triangle are of equal length.
The base angles of an isosceles triangle are equal.
An isosceles triangle has a line of symmetry that passes through the vertex and is perpendicular to the base.
The sum of the two non-right angles in a right-angled triangle is equal to 90 degrees.
The base angles are each half the measure of the exterior angle at the vertex.

Pythagorean_Theorem.definition: In a right triangle, the square of the length of the hypotenuse is equal to the sum of the squares of the lengths of the other two sides.
property: The square of the length of the hypotenuse is equal to the sum of the squares of the lengths of the other two sides.

Parallelogram.definition: A parallelogram is a type of quadrilateral with special properties; its opposite sides are parallel and equal in length.
property: Opposite sides are parallel: Each pair of opposite sides are parallel to each other (hence the name "parallelogram").
Opposite sides are equal in length: Both pairs of opposite sides are of equal length.
Opposite angles are equal: Both pairs of opposite angles are of equal measure.
Consecutive angles are supplementary: Each pair of angles on the same side of the parallelogram adds up to 180 degrees.
The diagonals bisect each other: The lines drawn between opposite corners (diagonals) cut each other in half.

Perpendicular_Diameter.definition: The diameter (or radius) that is perpendicular to a chord bisects the chord and also bisects the two arcs subtended by the chord.
property: Perpendicular to the chord.
Bisects the chord.
Bisects the arc.
In a triangle formed by a perpendicular diameter is a right-angled triangle.

Distance.definition: In a general sense, "distance" refers to the measure of how far apart two points are from each other.

Trigonometric_Functions.definition: Trigonometric functions are mathematical functions that relate the angles of a right-angled triangle to the lengths of its sides.
property: Sine (sin): For an angle in a right-angled triangle, the sine is the ratio of the length of the opposite side to the length of the hypotenuse.
Cosine (cos): For an angle in a right-angled triangle, the cosine is the ratio of the length of the adjacent side to the length of the hypotenuse.
Tangent (tan): For an angle in a right-angled triangle, the tangent is the ratio of the length of the opposite side to the length of the adjacent side.

Sum_Interior_Angles_Triangle.definition: The sum of the three angles in a triangle.
property: The sum of the measures of the interior angles of a triangle is always 180 degrees.

Parallel_Lines_Proportion.definition: When two lines are intersected by a set of parallel lines (at least three), the corresponding segments created on the lines are proportional in length.
property: The lengths of the corresponding segments formed are proportional.

Angle_Bisector.definition: An angle bisector is a line or ray that divides an angle into two congruent angles, each having half the measure of the original angle.
property: An angle bisector divides an angle into two equal parts.

Cyclic_Quadrilateral.definition: A cyclic quadrilateral is a quadrilateral (a four-sided polygon) where all four vertices lie on the circumference of a circle.
property: One of the key properties of a cyclic quadrilateral is that the sum of a pair of opposite angles is 180 degrees (supplementary).

Triangle_Exterior_Angle.definition: The exterior angle of a triangle is the angle that is adjacent to and supplementary to an interior angle of the triangle. It is formed when one side of the triangle is extended outwards, creating an angle outside the triangle with another side of the triangle.
property: The measure of an exterior angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of the measures of the two non-adjacent interior angles.

Triangle_Median.definition: The segment connecting the midpoints of two sides of a triangle is called the median of the triangle.
property: Parallel to the third side: The median is parallel to the third side of the triangle.
The length of the median is half the length of the third side of the triangle.

Circumcircle.definition: A circle that intersects with all the vertices of a polygon is called the circumscribed circle of the polygon.

Cone.definition: Mathematically, a right circular cone is a cone where the line segment (the altitude) connecting the apex to the center of the base is perpendicular to the base. This type of cone is commonly referred to simply as a "cone" and has a circular base.
property: The volume of a cone: V=1/3πr²h, where V is the volume, r is the radius of the base, and h is the height of the cone.
The base area of a cone: The area of the circular base of a cone is calculated using the formula: A_base = πr² , where A_base is the area of the base and r is the radius of the base.

The lateral surface area of a cone: The lateral surface area of a cone refers to the area of the conical surface that extends around the circumference of the base. It is calculated using the formula: A_lateral = πrl, where A_lateral is the lateral surface area, r is the radius of the base, and l is the slant height.

The surface area of a cone: The surface area of a cone includes both the base area and the lateral surface area. The formula is: A_surface = πr(r+l), where A_surface is the total surface area, r is the radius of the base, and l is the slant height.

Equilateral_Triangle.definition: An equilateral triangle is a polygon with three sides of equal length and three angles of equal measure. In an equilateral triangle.
property: All three sides are the same length (congruent).
All three interior angles are equal to each other and measure 60 degrees.

Perpendicular_Bisector.definition: A perpendicular bisector is a line or line segment that divides another line segment into two equal parts at a 90-degree angle.
property: Perpendicular: It intersects the line segment at a right angle (90 degrees).
Bisector: It cuts the line segment into two equal halves.
When two lines are parallel, the alternate interior angles formed are equal.

Circumcenter.definition: The circumcircle of a triangle is the unique circle drawn through the three vertices of the triangle. The circumcenter is the point of intersection of the perpendicular bisectors of the three sides of the triangle.
property: Perpendicular: The circumcenter is the point where the perpendicular bisectors of the three sides of the triangle intersect.
Equidistant property: The circumcenter is equidistant from the three vertices of the triangle.

Rhombus.definition: A rhombus (also known as a rhomb or diamond shape) is a special type of quadrilateral.
property: All four sides are of equal length.
Opposite angles are equal.
The diagonals bisect each other at right angles (90 degrees).
The diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other.
The diagonals of a rhombus bisect its angles, dividing one angle of the rhombus into two equal angles.

Slope_Angle.definition: The slope angle refers to the angle a line makes with the positive direction of the x-axis in a coordinate plane. This angle is typically measured in degrees or radians and indicates the steepness or incline of the line.
property: A slope angle of 0 degrees (or 0 radians) indicates a horizontal line.
A slope angle of 90 degrees (or π / 2 radians) indicates a vertical line.

Polygon.definition: A polygon is a two-dimensional geometric figure that consists of a finite number of straight line segments connected to form a closed chain or circuit.
property: The sum of the interior angles of a polygon depends on the number of sides it has. For a polygon with n sides, the sum of the interior angles is (n - 2) × 180 degrees.
Number of diagonals: A polygon with n sides can have n * (n - 3) / 2 diagonals.

Square.definition: A square is a special type of quadrilateral.
property: All four sides are of equal length (congruent).
All four interior angles are equal, each measuring 90 degrees (right angles).
The diagonals are equal in length and bisect each other at 90 degrees, meaning they are perpendicular.
The diagonals also bisect the angles of the square.
When two lines are parallel, the corresponding angles formed are equal.

Supplementary_Angles.definition: Two angles that share a common side, with their other sides being the extension of each other in opposite directions, are called adjacent supplementary angles.
property: The sum of the measures of two adjacent supplementary angles is always equal to 180 degrees.
All four interior angles are right angles, each measuring 90 degrees.
Opposite sides are parallel and equal in length.
Adjacent sides are perpendicular to each other.
The two diagonals of a rectangle are of equal length, and they bisect each other.

Symmetry.definition: Symmetry refers to a balanced and proportionate similarity found in two halves of an object, that is, one half is the mirror image of the other half.
property: In symmetrical shapes, corresponding lengths are equal.
In symmetrical shapes, corresponding angles are equal.

Sector.definition: A sector is a portion of a circle, bounded by two radii and the arc lying between them.
property: Area = (radius squared × central angle in degrees) ÷ 2. or Area = (π × radius squared × central angle in degrees) ÷ 360.

Rotation.definition: In geometry and mathematics, rotation refers to a transformation that turns a shape or a point around a fixed point, known as the center of rotation, through a specified angle and direction.
property: Angle-preserving property: Rotation does not change the measure of the angles within a shape, meaning that all angles of the figure remain unchanged before and after the rotation.
Distance-preserving property: Rotation does not alter the distance between any two points on a shape, meaning that the distance between any two points remains equal before and after the rotation.
The arc length created by rotation: (central angle × π × radius) / 180.

Right_Triangle_30_Angle.definition: A right-angled triangle with a 30-degree angle is a special type of right triangle where one angle measures 30 degrees and the other non-right angle measures 60 degrees.
property: Side length proportions: The length of the side opposite the 30-degree angle is half the length of the hypotenuse, while the length of the side opposite the 60-degree angle is √3 / 2 times the length of the hypotenuse.

Opposite_Angles.definition: Vertical angles are a pair of opposite angles formed by the intersection of two straight lines.
property: Equal in measure: The degrees of two vertical angles are equal.

Vertical.definition: A perpendicular line refers to two lines intersecting and being mutually perpendicular to each other.
property: Right angle: When a perpendicular line intersects with the line it is perpendicular to, the angle formed at the intersection is 90 degrees.

Direction_Angles.definition: Direction angles in mathematics, particularly in vector calculus and three-dimensional geometry, refer to the angles a vector makes with the positive axes of a coordinate system.
property: A direction angle is an angle that ranges from 0° to 360°, or from 0 to 2π radians

Direction angles can be used to determine the relative orientation between two vectors. For example, if the direction angles of two vectors differ by 180°, it indicates that their directions are opposite.
Direction angles can be calculated using trigonometric functions (such as sine, cosine, and tangent), or determined through the coordinates of a vector.

Translation.definition: In mathematics, particularly in geometry, a translation refers to a type of transformation that moves every point of a shape or object the same distance in the same direction.
property: Maintaining Shape and Size: A translation does not change the size and shape of a figure. The figure after translation is exactly the same as the original figure.
Lines Remain Parallel and Lengths Unchanged: In a figure, lines remain parallel and their lengths remain unchanged after translation.
Angles Remain Unchanged: The angles within a figure remain unchanged after translation.

Elevation_Angles.definition: Elevation angles are used to describe the angle between the horizontal plane and the line of sight directed upwards towards an object.
property: Range of Measurement: The elevation angle can range from 0 degrees (exactly at the horizontal level) to 90 degrees (directly overhead). An elevation angle of 0 degrees means the object is exactly at the observer's horizontal level, while 90 degrees means the object is directly above the observer.

Depression_Angles.definition: Depression angles refer to the angle formed below a horizontal line of sight.
property: Range of Measurement: Depression angles range from 0 degrees (exactly at the horizontal level) to -90 degrees (directly downward). A depression angle of 0 degrees indicates that the object is precisely on the observer's horizontal level, while -90 degrees would mean the object is directly beneath the observer.

Coordinates.definition: Coordinates are a set of values used to determine the position of a point or a geometric body within a particular space.
property: Slope: The slope, denoted as m, represents the degree of inclination of a line, and is calculated using the formula m = (y2 - y1) / (x2 - x1)
Equation of a Line: If there are two points (x1, y1) and (x2, y2), the equation of the line can be derived from these two points: y - y1 = m(x - x1).
Distance Formula: The distance d between two points can be calculated using the formula d = √((x2 - x1)² + (y2 - y1)²).
Midpoint Formula: The midpoint of two points (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) is ((x1 + x2) / 2, (y1 + y2) / 2).

Right_Triangle_Hypotenuse_Median.definition: The "Right Triangle Hypotenuse Median" refers to a specific line segment in a right triangle. It's the median line that connects the midpoint of the hypotenuse to the right angle vertex of the triangle.
property: Length Property: In a right triangle, the length of the median to the hypotenuse is exactly half the length of the hypotenuse.

Homothety.definition: Homothety, also known as a dilation, is a transformation in geometry that uniformly scales objects.
property: Similarity Ratio: There exists the same proportional relationship among all corresponding side lengths.
Equal Angles: Corresponding angles are equal.

Three-View_Drawing.definition: Standard Views: The three standard views are: Top View (Plan View): This shows the object as seen from above.  Front View (Elevation): This depicts the object as viewed from the front.  Side View (Profile View): Usually the right side, but sometimes the left side, this view shows the object as seen from its side.
property: Proportional Consistency: All views should be drawn to the same scale to ensure consistency and accuracy in dimensions among them.

Golden_Ratio.definition: The Golden Ratio is a mathematical ratio, commonly denoted by the Greek letter phi (Φ), with a value approximately equal to 1.618.
property: Mathematical Definition: If a line segment is divided into two parts, the long part (a) and the short part (b), then the Golden Ratio is achieved when the ratio of the whole segment (a+b) to the long part (a) is the same as the ratio of the long part (a) to the short part (b). Mathematically, it is expressed as: (a+b)/a = a/b = Φ.

Cylinder.definition: A cylinder is a three-dimensional geometric shape with straight parallel sides and a circular cross-section.
property: Volume: The volume of a cylinder can be calculated using the formula V = πr²h, where r is the radius of the base and h is the height.
Base Area: To calculate the base area of a cylinder, use the formula: A_base = πr², where A_base is the base area and r is the radius of the base.
Lateral Area: To calculate the lateral area of a cylinder, use the formula: A_lateral = 2πrh, where A_lateral is the lateral area, r is the radius of the base, and h is the height.

Surface Area: The surface area of a cylinder includes both the two bases and the lateral surface. The formula to calculate the surface area is: A_surface = 2πrh + 2πr², where A_surface is the surface area, r is the radius of the base circle, and h is the height of the cylinder.
